Hey, that's up JB is in the house for your weekly intensive. It's gonna be a great one great topic Wow, you know for those you want certification, you know, we're getting close to the end of the formal Training module by module right we're on looping and if you probably notice that I released half bit It's a really extra long module. And I can begin to tell you the level of Intracheasy the graphics because you know looping is almost like the whole straight line rolled up into one module It's crazy. So I think you guys are seeing that but it's a great lesson for me to really just kind of freeform one right now and kind of give you some thoughts of you know, non-traditional ways of explaining looping because you know in the Training the formal straight line certification. I can't possibly explain it better More intricately and more organized and I did there. It's just I mean literally, you know I spent probably I want to say six months on the looping module going back and forth that really that was like the one that was the the the piece to resistance and also that was the culmination of Using the book I wrote the way of the wolf as the template and that one chapter took me 60 days So it's like really I mean so you have with the certifications incredibly Logical step-by-step way of understanding the process of looping. So let me now go the other way I want to take you on the informal side. I really want to Encourage you to ask questions, especially with this module because one of the things I promised all of you guys on Certification said listen looping applies to any industry any business doesn't matter whether you're selling in person over the phone or flying online It doesn't matter looping is universal It's it's a mindset a methodology of how you go about handling people who hit you with the common objections, right? And I said if you are having trouble using that, you know, the pre-established patterns, they're just guidelines yet to be flexible, right? So I want to really make sure all your questions get answered I'm gonna save a little more and gonna kind of cut the formal training a bit short and really go on the Q&A for hopefully 45 I'll go for an hour on Q&A if you want as long as you keep asking me questions I'll keep going because I think it's that important. All right, so let me start with this informal way of explaining looping Imagine your Selling cars automobiles, right? You're an automobile salesman, right? And someone comes in and you know, they have basic knowledge of the car or they want to buy right? You go through a presentation, right? And then you know, you formally ask them for the order tell them how Much it'll cost how they get started be very simple you do the paperwork, right? And then they say let me think about it, right? Or let me call you back or I'm gonna shop still shopping around right one of the common objections, right? If you were to say to a client, well, let me ask you a question, you know, I hear what you're saying though But does the car make sense to you or do you like the car? Right, they'd look at you like I had three heads on Right, that's not what looping is. It's just not what all looping is It's this idea. It's a mindset a discipline that relates to the fact that Objections by their very nature are smoke screens for uncertainty That's it. It's it's all looping just understand the mindset It's based on the fact that Objections are smoke screens for uncertainty and in fact in the cert training that you haven't gotten this lesson It's less than 11 was a nine a nine or eleven I go through every single common objection and I show you How at the end of the day what it really is it's a smoke screen for some type of uncertainty Even if it's two levels down that I matter how you slice it if you really start thinking an objection through like you know And why is that and why do you feel that way you're always gonna get up because I'm not sure I'm not certain same thing right about one of the three tens always comes back there. So Looping represents this monumental breakthrough. I had 30 years ago that when I watched all my sales kids these kids right or kids, right and When they would get stuck in the back half of the sale they would tend to go into this back and forth of Getting hit with an objection Trying to overcome that objection and then try to ask for the order again And it never worked doesn't work Yet, I realized that that that magic moment that night when I invented the straight line And everything sort of coming clear to me. I was like, wait, wait a second. That's not what I do When I get hit with an objection Yeah, I'll always answer the objection except for the first one I deflect for other reasons right but the poison I'm in that objection handling phase Yeah, I had the greatest answers the greatest rebuttals to every single objection But never once never once Thanks, I love to speak so much. But never once would I just answer an objection and they go for the close It would be like in my own mind. It was like an impossible. Why would I do that? Because I know the objection tells me they still need to hear more. They have to learn more. They have to become more certain So when I answer an objection All it's doing is it's basically earning me the right to continue the conversation You know, they say you want to think about and I say I understand you want to think about it But you know, I'm just just one that you would use I'm doing this for a long time And you know, you're a busy guy. I'm a busy guy. I know you'll put it in the back of your mind You'll end up deciding against this not because you don't love the idea because I know you do and not because you don't need this That's because I know you need to you're just busy. You'll go back to your busy life I'll go back to mine will be like two shifts past the end of night and you'll miss out. I Don't want that to happen That's an answer to let me think about you know, say I want to think about well I understand that but it's just like I've been doing this for a while pal Like what I'm saying is like I'm doing this and you know every time someone says they want to think about it you know what I never hear from again and The sad part is is they they really need what I have I know they could use it it'll improve their lives and they know it too But we're busy with busy people assuming beings right the best time to make a decision Honestly is when all the facts are fresh in your mind and right now that's what's going right now all the facts are fresh in your mind now Which one I use of those those one is like all that you know, listen, I'm saying what I think about right now We just say some do this for quite some time and one thing I've learned is that when it comes to XYZ You know the best decisions are made when all the facts are right in front of you and fresh of your mind In fact, let me say this and that in fact, let me say this what that is is that you're said always your segue Into what really matters is building certainty There's no Answer to an objection is over a bottle That's gonna make them want to buy from you you have to get this through your head It's the most critical thing when it comes to people who was sitting on the fence And I and I let me just get up and do my beautiful artistry. Okay here. All right All right, you know we talk about I draw a fence. I'll be really This is a fence right The fence is a little chain-link fence right and he is you and you're sitting on the fence. That's you You're smiling you're on the fence right? What does that really mean right sitting on the fence to me is The holy grail of being an effective salesperson. In other words, what real sales people do is They take people who were sitting on the fence Meaning they're 50 50. They're not sure they want to buy They're on the fence. They have it made a decision. We take these people and somehow we get them to say yes now That to me is the essence of what a salesperson does now obviously there's all these things We have to do along the way to make that happen, but that in other words like if it's just about People who are really interested and you present to them and they buy You know, I mean honestly, I wouldn't pay someone a lot of money for that Like you know if I'm hiring sales people and I know that okay, listen, here's the deal guys This product sells itself. All you got to do is pick up the phone. You are selling You are a closing machine You're taking people who are on the fence and you're getting up to say yeah, let's do it right now And that requires you to persuade them to make them more certain about either the product logically emotionally You the salesperson that can trust and connect with you logically emotionally the company that stands behind the product logically emotionally If necessary lowering their action threshold meaning their belief systems they have about decision making about buying things that collective Mindset that says, you know what? Here's how certain I have to be All things being equal That all things being equals a very important statement. I just made how and I'm gonna tell you why I said so how Certain I have to be all things being equal before I'll say yeah, I I'm comfortable enough to buy So again, someone like me my all things being equal meaning, you know I don't have to be that certain if I kind of kind of like something. I'm like, yeah, you know what I like to buy stuff I like making quick decisions. I've always done well like that. I I trust my instincts. I trust my gut I don't think people are out to screw me I'm a half I'm a glass half full sort of guy my belief systems, right? Well, my father mad max ultra high action special needs the opposite You know, you gotta be really damn certain my father like damn certain No seeds of doubt or this guy is just he ain't buying My dad, as you know, is sick, right? And he's hanging in there because like I think he's like once he was too angry to die in his brother's life He's got too much pent up and angst He the grim reaper is scared of me still hanging on he's actually even proven which is awesome, right? I saw him last week. But you know, my dad's tough tough guy I'm a pushover compared to my dad when it comes to things like, you know, making decisions Negotiation I'm a very I'm an expert negotiator, but I come to the table to be friends I'm like, you know, looking at you mad at me in a pretty much positive light my dad's Who's out to screw me different belief systems, right? And that that's the essence of action to it. So all things being equal, right? If I kind of like something I'll buy it all things be equal my dad if you like something He won't buy it, right All things being equal, what's that mean? The absence of pain emergencies Bad things happening. You see that all things being equal means in the absence of some event In the absence of an urgency where I gotta do something because I'm you know, either I'm in pain or I'm worried or uncle In the once that enters the equation all bets are off I don't care who you are how tough a buyer you are if you're feeling immediate massive pain over something You will take action to resolve that pain your action threshold temporarily drops through the floor And you buy to try to get out of pain and put yourself back in a feeling if not pleasure at least a feeling of being okay again We don't want to walk around feeling like crap. That's not how human beings are built Now How do we take all this so I kind of I gave you these five things really it was five points in there, right? I said what they got to be certain about the product and we'll combine logical emotion to one thing, right? Certain about you the sales person said about the company those are three things of three tens, right basic stuff, right? I then said the beliefs that you have this sort of half Full half empty glass Who are you dealing with? What are the beliefs about buying? What are the beliefs about making decisions? What are the beliefs about? bearing risk What are the beliefs about generally like, you know, am I capable Of making a good decision on my own do I need others to advise me like there's all these deep-seated beliefs That that are Engrained in us. They've been formed Since we were very small by things that our parents said by things that we experienced ourselves things people said to us in passing things we saw on tv Things we heard out of the corner of our ear And most importantly things that happened to us when they impacted us in a very painful way Those things formed very very strong beliefs and and they are usually limiting beliefs Like, you know, you make that decision It's like in that in that moment when you're feeling bad And it hits you like oh, that's that's how limiting beliefs get formed You know like with me we're not taking action to start my business when I was 23 I delayed starting stratton at first because of that one failure in the meat business one failure After eight or nine successes and as an entrepreneur when I was a kid onto 22 years old I had eight even more businesses One failure and I literally said myself. Maybe I'm not entrepreneurial That's it. That's what looping is I sold you I gave I gave it to college straw. You said let me think about it I'm gonna sell you again, and I'm gonna sell you again Now the problem is is if you try to do that Just do it you piss people off You break rapport with them You push them away So we can't do that And of course I realized that as a natural born closer it occurred to me I was like, all right. Well, I know I gotta resell them. They're not sold enough yet. They're just not they're not sold enough They still need to be sold They're not sold But I just can't go out and sell them because they'll be pissed at me It's like I'm it's a confrontation. They say let me think about it. Let me just sell you again. It doesn't work that way So looping is this sort of dance that you do with the prospect That allows you to keep reselling him again or again And again and again without pissing them off without breaking rapport Without them feeling they've become disempowered. They're you're punching bad like you're not hearing me That you're not gonna get very fall like that. Like, you know, they say let me think about you're like, well, listen Let me let me just tell you no you you can't just keep going That's why I'm so this you know, there's a lot of sales programs out there And and and and by the way, they're not all bad Some of these some of them, right? They're okay. Some of them. Nothing's great, but there's some that are okay But it's interesting. They like there's some decent features Yet when it comes to this thing, they all miss it. They miss the boat on it It's like they don't understand the the the The toxic nature of a confrontation with your client Or your prospect you can't get into a test of wills with someone This is like there's an overriding factor That yes, I have to resell them. I gotta make them more certain But if at any point in time I reveal myself to be a novice Or I bore them Or I come off as a hungry salesperson just wants commission Or they don't care. I break break rapport Any one of those things happens like It's like death switch. It's not and by the way, it's not this is not death from a thousand cuts One cut you're dead These are knockout blows you break rapport. Are you really break reports and knockout blow You're not going to recover from that in the back end of the sale if if you are perceived In the looping section as someone you're just doing because you want to make money You're not going to close the person You're not there's not and any amount of loops in the world that can offset that positioning We must be perceived As someone who cares Who's on their side who wants to help them get what they want and by the way, this better be true I'm not like trying to say this in an evil way. This is what you're doing. This is what ethical persuasion is about You're helping someone resolve a problem You're helping them cure a pain point Nothing wrong with that so Question Given I I think I've kind of really beaten this dead horse as much as I can right now But I think you get it right you get what's going on looping is just simply a methodology That allows you to keep reselling someone without pissing them off That's lame in terms to keep selling without pissing people off for as long as it takes until they buy Or the conversation ends and I get into that in lesson 11 or 12 right well 10 11 12, right? All right. The first one's from donna kundi Here's my question for jb. I don't know if it's been discussed yet But is there a way to use the straight line when selling from stage? In a one to many situations, of course, of course, of course, listen I did a ton of selling from station. I really dislike it. I'll tell you why for me. Okay. Um, I had never sold from stage before And and by the way, so what's the first thing I do? I went out and hired the best people to show me what they did. So a third loop Would be and again, so there's no okay So, you know, it's not set in stone, right? But typically your your first pattern Right, you ask for the order and the prospect says let me think about it You forget hit with the first objection, right? So you deflect that first objection you move back into presentation mode You resell the product and you run your forest gun pattern Resell yourself the company that stands behind the product then you ask for the order again Now that's not to say that you can't you could also lower the action threshold as well You could do it in your first pad. It depends what you're selling what the circumstances if the person's in a rush Which is how you feel so there's no it's not set remember flexibility is king here The idea is that your ultimate. Let's look at your ultimate loop Your ultimate loop would be where you loop back you answer you so you always get a deflect the first objection, right? Let's say you're on your second objection your ultimate kitchen sink loop you loop back You resell the product logically and emotionally You resell yourself logically and emotionally You resell the company that stands behind that lot product logically emotionally You then lower their action threshold and then you add on pain And then you take him into a soft close Obeying the energy in benefits out equation and you ask for the order that's your kitchen sink loop, right? You have everything in there. You have pain addition action threshold lowering pattern three tens logically and emotionally In the real world what we try to do is we you know, we try to be elegant and not just dump things So we'll I'll hold hardest Elements four and five I'm not gonna lower action threshold until I firstly let me just see if I can you know many times I found and this experience I have just that simply one basic loop of you know Making them more certain about the product running my far as gum pattern Transitioning into the company. I found that honestly 25 percent of the time or more you can I'll close them right there with that one pattern Boom just like that now necessary if If it calls for if it's allowed meaning the product I'm selling allows me to I'll engage in a step down sale as well Meaning if I start off I'll ask for 10,000 of whatever it is and then I'll say you want your stocks more at five that's like I could also step down So what do you do your third loop? Well, well according to the the textbook, you know the textbook loop Yeah, your third your first loop would be Just far as come pattern your second loop your lowering action threshold And your third loop you're adding on pain, but remember And if you're in certification, you'll know this and you go through it tonight, right or tomorrow morning is that We always even when we're lowering the action we're always buttoning up certainty as well So as you go down Further and further into your looping process, you're always doing the first three and the fourth thing You're buttoning things up with certainty ways lowering the action threshold ways pain way Wains means you have to always button it up.
